title: The Hermit's Clearing (Sample)
author: Book of Infinite Tales
description: A sample book demonstrating all schema features — response/resolution passages, encounter picker, and game structure.

entries.title: The Golden Age of Camelot Begins
entries.body:
The feast of Pentecost fills the great hall of Camelot with light and laughter. King Arthur surveys his assembled knights with a father's pride. A season of adventure lies ahead.

Go forth. The realm awaits your deeds.

entries.title: The Quest of the Holy Grail Begins
entries.body:
A blinding light has filled the hall. The Holy Grail has appeared and vanished in an instant, leaving every knight shaken and afire with longing. One by one, the great knights of the Round Table vow to seek the sacred vessel.

The roads of Britain stretch before you. The Grail is somewhere in the world.

entries.title: A Clearing in the Wood
entries.body:
You come upon a mossy clearing. A hermit tends a small fire, stirring a pot that smells of thyme and iron. He looks up without surprise, as though he has been expecting you.

"Sit, traveller," he says, "or do not. The choice is yours."

responses.label: You may sit and share his meal, making conversation.
You may ask about the strange markings carved into the stones around his fire.
You may leave the hermit to his fire and ride on.

entries.title: A Shared Meal
entries.body: You lower yourself beside the fire. The hermit passes you a wooden bowl of broth. The silence is not unfriendly, but it wants filling.

resolutions.label: speak plainly of your road and your doubts

success.body:
Your honesty opens him like a book left too long in the dark. He tells you of a stag he once saw with a cross between its antlers, and how it turned him from a life of violence to one of contemplation.

When you look up from your bowl, the fire is out and he is gone. A bone comb lies where he sat.

failure.body: Your words come out heavier than you meant — too much of your own glory, too many half-truths. The hermit grows quiet and stares into the fire as though you are not there. After a time you rise and leave in silence.

resolutions.label: charm him with tales of your adventures

success.body:
You weave a story so fine the hermit laughs aloud — a sound, you sense, he has not made in years. He presses a small carved token into your palm.

"A gift," he says, "for the man who gave me back my laugh."

failure.body: The hermit listens politely, but his eyes never leave the fire. When you finish, he nods once and goes on stirring. You eat the rest of the meal feeling very small.

entries.title: The Carved Stones
entries.body: You kneel beside the nearest stone and trace the carvings with one finger. The hermit watches with bright eyes, saying nothing.

resolutions.label: study the signs by their shape and weathering

success.body:
You know these signs — older than the Church, older than Rome. A prayer against wandering spirits. The hermit's expression does not change, but something in his posture eases.

He hands you a sealed clay jar. "For the road," he says. "Do not open it until you have great need."

failure.body:
You study the carvings. They might be letters, or animals, or simply the work of a bored knife on a winter night. The hermit gently takes your arm and guides you back to the fire.

"Some things are not yet yours to read," he says without unkindness.

entries.title: Back to the Road
entries.body:
The forest thins. The road is where you left it, but the light has changed — it is later than it should be. You do not look back.

(End of encounter.)

entries.title: The Hermit — Amorous
entries.body:
The hermit tends his fire with the unhurried patience of a man who expects nothing from the world. He glances up as you approach, and a knowing look crosses his weathered face.

"You are not here for spiritual counsel," he says. "Your heart is full of something earthly."

responses.label: You may confess the name of the one who occupies your thoughts.
You may deny it and ask for his blessing anyway.

entries.title: A Confession
entries.body: You speak the name, and the saying of it lightens your chest. The hermit stirs his pot and is quiet for a while.

resolutions.label: ask if your love is foolish or worthy

success.body: "All love is foolish," the hermit says. "The worthy kind is the kind that does not ask to be returned." He ladles broth into a bowl and hands it to you. You sit together in silence until the stars appear.

failure.body: The hermit looks at you for a long moment. "I am the wrong man to ask," he says at last. "I gave all that up." He returns to his fire, and you feel the distance between his life and yours.

entries.title: A Denial
entries.body: You assume a solemn expression and ask for his blessing. The hermit studies you without expression for several seconds, then raises one eyebrow.

success.body: He gives you a long blessing in a tongue you do not recognise, and lays his hand briefly on your head. As you rise to leave, he says without looking up: "Give my regards to whoever it is."

failure.body: Your face betrays you. The hermit snorts with laughter — the first laughter you've seen from him — and it softens into a kinder sound. He blesses you anyway, briefly, between chuckles.

entries.title: The Hermit — Lost
entries.body:
You have been riding in circles for the better part of an hour when you smell woodsmoke and follow it to a hermit's fire. He does not seem surprised to see you.

"You have the look of a man who has lost something," he says, "besides his way."

responses.label: You may admit you are lost and ask for directions.
You may say only that you are passing through.

entries.title: Honestly Lost
entries.body: You admit it freely — the road, the landmarks, your purpose for coming this way. The hermit listens without interruption.

resolutions.label: describe what you were looking for when you lost your way

success.body: The hermit draws you a map in the dirt with a stick: three turns, a ford, a distinctive oak. "The ford runs fast today," he adds, "but your horse looks steady." You ride out of the clearing with the road clear in your mind.

failure.body: You explain at some length, and the hermit listens at some length, and at the end of it he says: "I have not been out of this clearing in eleven years. I cannot help you." He returns to his pot. You leave no wiser but somehow less troubled.

entries.title: A Vague Claim
entries.body:
The hermit nods slowly and says nothing for a while. Then he points with his stirring stick toward a gap in the trees.

"That way," he says. "Wherever it is you're passing through."

entries.title: The Hermit's Clearing — A Place of Power (Age 1)
entries.body:
You have returned to the clearing, drawn by a sense that your previous visit left something unfinished. The fire is cold. A circle of carved stones surrounds it, and you understand now that this is no ordinary hermit's camp.

You kneel at the centre of the circle and wait.

resolutions.label: open yourself to whatever the place holds

success.body:
A warmth rises through the stone beneath your knees. You hear — or perhaps feel — a voice that speaks without words, leaving in its wake a certainty you cannot explain and will not be able to convey to anyone. But you carry it forward.

(Place 1 Destiny Token on this Location Card.)

failure.body:
You kneel until your legs ache. Nothing comes. The clearing is just a clearing, and the stones are just stones. You rise stiffly and ride away, not quite sure what you were hoping for.

(Place 1 Destiny Token on this Location Card.)

entries.title: The Hermit's Clearing — A Place of Power (Age 2)
entries.body:
The clearing has changed. The carved stones glow faintly in the grey afternoon, and the fire that burned here before has left an ash circle that no rain has disturbed. You sense that the Grail's passage through the world has changed places like this one.

You kneel again at the centre, with more knowledge than before, and more to lose.

resolutions.label: seek a vision of the Grail's path

success.body:
The stones pulse once, then settle. Behind your closed eyes you see a road, a river, a tower on a hill — and in the tower, a light. You will know the place when you reach it.

(Place 1 Destiny Token on this Location Card.)

failure.body:
Nothing comes. The Grail has moved on, and this place has quieted. You leave with the uncomfortable sense that the window has passed.

(Place 1 Destiny Token on this Location Card.)

entries.title: Strange Beast — Forest
entries.body: Something large moves in the trees ahead of you — too large for a deer, too quiet for a boar. You rein in your horse and wait. A pair of eyes catches the light between the branches, blinking slowly.

success.body: The creature steps into a shaft of light — a great hound, pale as milk, with red-tipped ears. A fairy hound, away from its master. It regards you without fear, then turns and disappears into the trees, leaving a single silver antler-tip on the ground where it stood.

failure.body: A branch snaps under your horse's hoof. Whatever it was crashes away through the undergrowth at speed, and the forest goes silent. You find only a scuff in the leaf mould where something heavy stood.

entries.title: Strange Beast — Mountain
entries.body: High on the slope above the treeline, you have found the bones. A circle of them — large, heavy bones, from no animal you can name — arranged with what looks like deliberate intention around a flat stone.

resolutions.label: examine them with what you know of such things

success.body: They are old — very old — and they have been placed here, not dropped. A shrine. Whatever creature these bones belonged to was venerated by something, and perhaps still is. You make the sign of the cross and leave a small offering. As you descend, the wind at your back feels like a hand pushing you forward.

failure.body: You study the bones and come to no useful conclusion. The arrangement might be natural. The stone might be coincidence. You ride on, unsatisfied, with a slight chill that has nothing to do with the altitude.

entries.title: Strange Beast — Plains
entries.body: Your retinue sees it first — a shadow passing across the empty plain faster than any shadow should move without a cloud to cast it. Then it is gone, and the grass lies flat in a long oval where something enormous rested moments ago.

resolutions.label: track where the shadow came from

success.body: The flattened grass leads you to a circle of disturbed earth and, at the centre, a single object: a bronze clasp, ancient, engraved with a serpent eating its own tail. One of your retainers whispers a prayer. You pocket the clasp and ride on quickly.

failure.body: The grass gives up nothing useful. Your retainers are unhappy. You spend the next hour riding in the wrong direction before the road reasserts itself.

entries.title: Strange Beast — City
entries.body: Three people in the market square are pointing upward and speaking in low voices. On the roof of the guild hall crouches something that looks, from a distance, like a very large cat. From closer, it does not look like a cat at all.

success.body: You ride forward at a steady pace — not charging, not retreating — and the creature on the roof watches you with yellow eyes. After a long moment it drops to the alley below and is gone, leaving claw-marks in the lead roof. The market crowd cheers. The city's alderman presses a purse into your hand.

failure.body: Your horse shies and will not be controlled. By the time you have it settled, the creature has gone, and so have several market stalls that stood in its path. The alderman gives you a look.

entries.title: Strange Beast — Forest (Age 2)
entries.body:
The forest has grown strange since the Grail appeared and vanished. Something moves in the shadows that does not belong here — or perhaps it has always been here, and the Grail's light has simply made it visible.

A shape circles you just beyond sight.

resolutions.label: call out to it in the old names

success.body: It stops. After a long silence, something lays a single white feather on the path before you and is gone. The feather is dry, though it has been raining. You take it as a sign.

failure.body: The old names produce nothing but echo. Whatever moves in these trees answers to different words now, if it answers at all. You ride through quickly.

entries.title: Strange Beast — Mountain (Age 2)
entries.body: High on the mountain, you find the creature resting — if resting is the right word. It is very large, very still, and it watches you with the patience of something that has been waiting for a very long time. Its eyes hold no malice. Only expectation.

resolutions.label: sit before it and await its will

success.body: You sit for what feels like an hour. The creature lowers its enormous head until it nearly touches the ground before you, then rises, turns, and walks into the cloud. The stone where it rested is warm beneath your hand, and will remain warm until morning.

failure.body: You last perhaps twenty minutes before your nerve breaks. The creature watches you leave without expression.

entries.title: Strange Beast — Plains (Age 2)
entries.body: Across the wide plain, something is running. It is fast — faster than a horse — and it is heading directly toward you.

success.body: It veers at the last moment and passes you like a storm, close enough to feel the wind of it. In its wake, coins lie scattered on the plain — old coins, gold, from no mint you know. Your retinue fills their pockets in reverent silence.

failure.body: You flinch. So does your horse, and so do your retainers. The creature runs straight through your formation and is gone before anyone can respond. No one is hurt, but no one speaks for several minutes.

entries.title: Strange Beast — City (Age 2)
entries.body: The city is subdued. Word has reached them of the Grail, and of other things seen on the roads since — things that have no place in the ordinary world. The creature you have tracked to the city's edge sits in plain sight by the gate, and the guards will not approach it.

success.body: You address it as you would address a lord whose language you do not fully speak — respectfully, without looking directly at it. After a pause it moves aside. The guards open the gates. The city alderman gives you the freedom of the city before you've dismounted.

failure.body: It ignores everything you say. After an embarrassing length of time, it simply wanders off of its own accord. The gates open. You ride in to the sound of unconvinced applause.

entries.title: Epilogue — The Clearing at the End
entries.body:
When it is over, you find yourself thinking of the hermit.

Not of the kings, nor the battles, nor the vision of the Grail — but of the old man and his fire in the clearing, the way he stirred his pot as though the fate of the realm were none of his business. You wonder if he is still there. You wonder if he was ever really there at all.

Britain will have other stories. Other knights will ride out in other ages, looking for their hermits in the woods. You hope they find them.

You hope the fire is warm.

entries.title: Seek the Hermit's Blessing — Quest Complete
entries.body:
You have returned to the hermit's clearing with what he asked of you. He examines the object in his hands for a long time, nodding slowly.

"It is as I hoped," he says. "Sit. I will give you what I promised."

He speaks a blessing in words older than Latin, and when he finishes, something has shifted — in the air, in you, in the quality of the afternoon light. You do not know what has changed. You know only that it has.
